h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I did complete every item on the sheet, so I thought that meant it was ready for sign-off.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Sorry, I think I was using the completed sheet as cover for the decision.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I did complete every item on the sheet, so I thought that meant it was ready for sign-off.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I doubled the side with the ruler, so I was calling that double the square
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is trying to reconcile why doubling the side length with a ruler results in four squares on the grid instead of two, viewing it as an annoying contradiction in their evidence.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Then the evidence has turned against that claim. It is not a drawing trick. Say the two claims separately.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor responds to the learner's frustration by separating the side and area claims to systematically analyze the contradiction.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Use that replacement. Side claim: not doubled. Area claim: count it. This is two old squares. Shape claim: fails. It is two across and one up, not a square.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor immediately adopts the learner's 'replacement' framing to test the new configuration of two old squares.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I doubled the side with the ruler, so I was calling that double the square

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Use that replacement. Side claim: not doubled. Area claim: count it. This is two old squares. Shape claim: fails. It is two across and one up, not a square.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor immediately adopts the learner's 'replacement' framing to test the new configuration of two old squares.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Then the evidence has turned against that claim. It is not a drawing trick. Say the two claims separately.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor responds to the learner's frustration by separating the side and area claims to systematically analyze the contradiction.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Then the evidence has turned against that claim. It is not a drawing trick. Say the two claims separately.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor responds to the learner's frustration by separating the side and area claims to systematically analyze the contradiction.
